topic: CMB Axis of Evil - verification of dup-sentence removal (queue_id 350)
our_argument_summary: WIN-048 (CMB Axis of Evil) is labelled Self-Contradicted under EXP-295. The structural pillars are: (a) false-dilemma framing of LambdaCDM anomalies as dome evidence, (b) eroded Planck reanalysis significance, (c) WIN-034 cast-copper-firmament/160 GHz opacity self-contradiction. The c6 detail_extra closing paragraph repeated the one-line summary 'Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper' twice - once inside the numbered list at item 3, once in the closing prose. The queue push asks me to confirm the dup-removal landed cleanly and that the parallel ISS-2008 routing of the moderate sub_rationale hole is in place.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py domain handles CMB Axis of Evil. Only artifact is a static live-power dashboard tile (Predicted: >2sigma, Observed: 2.5sigma, Tolerance: N/A). No fetch, no comparison. Unchanged from c2-c6.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Display-only static threshold against a known result.
data_sources_used: WMAP / Planck PR3/PR4 CMB temperature maps via published literature - not fetched in code
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None present in monitor.py because the WIN has no monitor.py entry.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward from c2-c6 with no change - no monitor.py domain, only a static dashboard tile (data/wins.json WIN-048.detail_verdict_text still documents this verbatim).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward - the dome adopts the anomaly via the LambdaCDM-counter-prediction framing without offering a different numerical value; no renamed-mechanism pattern.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward - Land & Magueijo 2005 (PRL 95, 071301) and Planck PR3/PR4 maps were public well before any dome monitor commit; detail_verdict_text continues to cite these.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward - no derivation chain from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude / moon_altitude to any CMB observable; detail_evidence still explicitly notes no analog in dome geometry.

verification_results.queue_push_reason: WIN-048 dup sentence removed (detail_extra), ISS-2008 sub_rationale inconsistency created (assigned-analyst). Verification pass to confirm dup removal landed cleanly.
verification_results.iss_2008_note: Moderate sub_rationale inconsistency across MOND-grouping batch (WIN-047/048/052/054/055) correctly externalised - not restated in c7 holes_found.
verification_results.iss_2009_note: Minor SC pattern (2) gloss/anchor still pending - decider has not self-applied yet but ticket is filed. Not restated in c7 holes_found.

holes_found.description: Punctuation artifact at the dup-sentence removal seam in detail_extra: list item 3 ends with two consecutive periods - '(contradicting WIN-048 premise)..' - visible in the rendered HTML. The trailing extra period is a stub left over from when the duplicate 'Lowering the statistical significance...' sentence was excised; the original sentence terminated the list item with its own period, and only the duplicate text was removed, leaving the period attached to the preceding sentence-final period. Cosmetic, not load-bearing, but visible to careful readers (data/wins.json WIN-048.detail_extra, char ~2624).
holes_found.recommendation: Trivial single-character patch: remove one period so list item 3 ends with a single period and the / close cleanly. Decider can self-apply (severity minor, single-byte edit).

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c6 moderate hole #0 (sub_rationale cross-WIN inconsistency across MOND-grouping batch - WIN-047 cites Part 6.14, WIN-048/052/054 cite Part 6.13, WIN-055 has no sub_rationale) is now externalised to ISS-2008 (status assigned-analyst). This c7 review is verification-class and is not restating the moderate hole in holes_found - tracked via ISS-2008. holes_index -1 because the carry-over is in the issue tracker, not restated in this review JSON.

c6 minor hole #2 (SC pattern (2) reasoning undefined on first mention in detail_evidence) is now externalised to ISS-2009 (status open). Not restated in c7 holes_found - tracked via ISS-2009. holes_index -1 by same convention.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dup-removal patch was the right scope. Rather than blanket-rewriting the closing paragraph, the decider chose the surgical excision of the duplicate inside list item 3 and preserved the closing prose summary (data/wins.json WIN-048.detail_extra closing sentence, ~3.3 KB, length verified this cycle).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Verification of patch hygiene is not a substantive defense of WIN-048 itself; the three structural failures (false-dilemma framing, eroded Planck significance, WIN-034 copper-opacity self-contradiction) all remain (data/wins.json WIN-048.detail_evidence paragraphs 1-3, unchanged from c6). The patch addresses cosmetic redundancy only.

deeper_analysis:
The other side: a fair dome-side reading of the patch sequence is that we crowd-source dup-finding via the curmudgeon and the patches we receive are mechanical, not substantive - i.e., the verification queue is busy-work. The counter to that is what this c7 documents: the queue item is genuinely closing a c6 finding (dup_sentence_occurrences_after_patch=1, down from 2), and the parallel routing through ISS-2008 / ISS-2009 keeps the moderate and the second minor on a separate track so the verification class can be discharged independently. That is the system working as designed: minor patches do not pile onto the substantive ticket queue and do not block heavy-rotation re-reviews on other WINs.

Advocate-mode check: a dome defender reading this WIN today would still see the same load-bearing structural critique (WIN-034 copper-firmament opacity at 160 GHz, false-dilemma framing, no dome CMB mechanism) and the same secondary critique that the dome author has himself revised the sigma claim downward. The patches between c6 and c7 do not change any of that. If the defender argues we are overweight on the WIN-034 link, that is a substantive challenge - but it is a substantive challenge, not a verification challenge, and ISS-2008's MOND-grouping routing already opens a venue for re-examining the cross-WIN inferential pattern under EXP-295.

citation_check.note: All three DOIs verified in earlier cycles (c2-c6). This c7 is a verification-class re-review of a specific patch hygiene event; no new citations introduced, no need to re-verify.

summary_for_decider: Verification pass for queue_id 350 (WIN-048 dup-sentence removal). Patch landed cleanly: detail_extra shrank by 76 chars (3414 -> 3338), the duplicated 'Lowering the statistical significance does not change the opacity of copper' sentence now appears exactly once (in the closing summary paragraph, as intended). One small cosmetic side-effect: list item 3 ends with two periods (..) rather than one - single-byte patch, severity minor, decider can self-apply. ISS-2008 (moderate, assigned-analyst) and ISS-2009 (minor, open) confirmed present and correctly route the unaddressed c6 findings. Verdict Self-Contradicted unchanged; structural pillars intact. recommended_action: no_change for the WIN itself; one minor hole filed for the trailing-period artifact.
